*** 5 INJ *** NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED A EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 2 MILES NORTH OF CRANESVILLE AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES. THE TORNADO HIT A MOBILE HOME WHERE 5 PEOPLE WERE INJURED. TREE DAMAGE WAS ALSO OBSERVED ALONG THE TORNADO PATH. WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH.
STRUCTURE DAMAGE TO HOUSES AND DOWN POWER LINES.

*** 2 INJ *** NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 2 MILES NORTH OF EDINBORO AND WAS ON THE GROUND INTERMITTENTLY FOR 18 MILES AS IT TRACKED EAST THROUGH UNION CITY. CONSIDERABLE TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED WITH MULTIPLE TREES FALLING ON HOMES. WINDS ALSO CAUSED DAMAGE TO ROOFS AND SIDING. ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS WERE 100-105 MPH.
MOBILE HOME DESTROYED IN WASHINGTON TOWNSHIP BY POSSIBLE TORNADO. REPORTED BY PEMA.
DAMAGE IN THE CITY OF UNION CITY BY POSSIBLE TORNADO. REPORTED BY PEMA.

REPORTS OF A POSSIBLE MICROBURST NEAR THE NORWALK RESERVOIR. FOUR 18 INCH DIAMETER TREES WERE UPROOTED. A HALF DOZEN ADDITIONAL TREES WERE DAMAGED WITH DOWNED LIMBS. ALL DAMAGE DESCRIBED OCCURRED WITHIN A 10 YARD RADIUS.
ONE TREE DOWN IN THE TWIN LAKES AREA

RAINFALL MEASURED BETWEEN 4 AND 5 INCHES FELL OVER MCKEAN TOWNSHIP PRODUCING WIDESPREAD FLOODING ALONG AREA ROADS. ELK CREEK CAME OUT OF ITS BANKS AND INUNDATED A BASEMENT AND GARAGE ALONG RICK ROAD. HAMOT ROAD WAS WASHED OUT ON ONE SIDE REQUIRING ROAD CREWS TO MAKE REPAIRS. WATER UP TO THREE FEET DEEP WAS REPORTED ON ROADS.

POSSIBLE TORNADO REPORTED ALONG ROUTE 281.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONFIRMS AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 1 MILE WEST OF CUSTAR. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 0.9 MILES AS IT TRACKED TO THE EAST WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. THE TORNADO HAD A MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 80 MPH. THE TORNADO CUASED MINOR DAMAGE TO DOORS AND ROOFS OF TWO HOMES AND A GARAGE ALONG WITH MANY DOWNED TREES.
